questions.questionText: Defines how devices are uniquely identified and how data is routed across a network. It is the most widely used addressing scheme in modern networking.
An IPv4 is ___-bit.
The 32 bits are divided into four 8-bit sections. Each can have a decimal value ranging from 0 to 255.
For computers to process the address, each decimal number is converted into 8-bits of binary.

Designed for massive organizations like governments or global ISPs.

- First octet range: 1 - 126
- Default subnet mask: 255.0.0.0

Designed for large companies or universities.

- First octet range: 128 - 191
- Default subnet mask: 255.255.0.0
- Supports about 16,000 networks and 65,534 host networks.
127 is used for _________.

Designed to connect small networks, such as homes or offices, allowing up to 254 devices to communicate.

- First octet range: 192 - 223
- Default subnet mask: 255.255.255.0
- Supports over 2 million networks, but only 254 hosts per network.
This class is not assigned to specific hosts. Instead, it is used for sending data from source to a specific group of recipients simultaneously
Reserved by the IETF for research, testing, or future use. You will almost never see these in a live production environment.
Refers to a 32-bit number that works side-by-side with an IP address to determine which part of the address belong to the network and which part belongs to the host (the specific device).
An IP like 192.168.1.0/26, the /26 tells you that the first 26 bits are ___________ (set to 1).
Every device needs a unique _____________.
Manually typed in by an administrator (common for servers and printers).
Automatically assigned by a router when a device joins the network.
_________________________ is the IP address of the router interface that connects your local network to the outside world (the internet).
If a computer wants to send data to an IP that is not in its own subnet, it sends that data to the Default Gateway.
Computers only understand numbers (e.g. 142.250.190.47). Translates it into human readable text.
Allows a network administrator to take on physical switch and chop into multiple, private VIRTUAL switches.
3 Approach in trouble-shooting in network faults.
Start at Layer 1 (Cables) and move up to Layer 7. When you suspect a hardware or wiring issue.
Start at Layer 7 (Apps) and move down to Layer 1. When only one specific app (like CHrome) isn't working.
Start at Layer 3 or 4 (Ping/Traceroute). To quickly see if the problem is hardware or software.

Software tools:
- Ping (_________)
- Traceroute (____________)
- Ipconfig / all

Hardware tools:
- ___________ Plug
- Tone ___________ and Probe (Fax and Hound)

7 Step troubleshooting process.

Professional engineers follow this strict cycle to ensure they don't make the problem worse:

1. Identify the ______________.
2. Establish a ____________.
3. Test the _____________.
4. Establish a ___________.
5. Implement a ___________.
6. Verify _____________.
7. Document ___________.
questions.answer: Problem, Theory, Theory, Plan, Solution, Functionality, Findings

questions.questionText: The CIA Triad.
Software specifically designed to disrupt, damage, or gain unauthorized access to a computer system.
The art of manipulating people into giving up confidential information.
The discovery and mapping of systems, services, or vulnerabilities.
Attempts to gain unauthorized access to accounts or data.
An attack meant to shut down a machine or network, making it inaccessible to users.

name: ITPS206 Finals Topic 3B - Basic Protection Techniques

questions.questionText: Only the intended recipient should be able to read the data. This is achieved through encryption, access controls, and hiding data.
Data must remain 'clean' and unchanged from the moment it is sent to the moment it is received.
Systems must be "up" whenever they are needed. This involves redundancy (having backups) and maintenance.
Protecting the devices at the edge (laptops, phones, tablets). This includes Antivirus and Host-based firewalls.
Making a device as secure as possible by removing unnecessary features.
The __________ is a common model that forms the basis for the development of security systems. They are used for finding vulnerabilities and methods for creating solutions.

Refer to any potential danger to network integrity, confidentiality, or availability. These threats manifest as unauthorized access attempts, data interception, service disruptions, or infrastructure compromises.
____________________ range from opportunistic cybercriminals to state-sponsored groups with advanced capabilities and resources.
A set of "IF/THEN" rules on a router if traffic comes from the Student VLAN and if it's trying to go to the Server Room, then Block it.
A barrier between your "Trusted" internal network and the "Untrusted" internet. It inspects every packet.
It creates an encrypted "tunnel" over the public internet, making a remote user appear as if they are sitting in the office.
The baseline for modern networking. It supports speeds up to 1 Gbps at 100 MHz.
Features tighter twists and often a plastic separator (spline) to reduce crosstalk. It supports 10 Gbps for distances up to 55 meters.
Designed to support 10Gbps over the full 100-meter cable length by increasing frequency to 500 MHz.

name: ITPS206 Finals Topic 4B - Cable Types and Pinouts

questions.questionText: Both ends of the cable use the same wiring standard (usually T568B to T568B).
One end is T658A and the other is T568B.
One end is T658B and the other is T568A.
